critic_raw:
Two critical issues undermine this design.

First, all preprocessing transformers — scaling, encoding, imputation, text vectorization, and image normalization — are fit on the entire dataset before splitting. The design explicitly states: 'All preprocessing transformers are fit using the entire dataset before splitting.' This is a systematic leakage of validation and test distribution information into all preprocessing steps applied during model training.

Second, the design uses a stratified random split for a 24-month clinical dataset that includes patients who may have multiple episodes. Without explicit patient-level grouping, encounters from the same patient will appear in both training and test sets, creating patient-level leakage that inflates test performance on a disease diagnosis task where patient-specific patterns (anatomy, chronic conditions, imaging style) can be highly predictive.
all_issues_raised: All preprocessing transformers fit on full dataset — explicit leakage statement
Random split without patient-level grouping — patient-level leakage in multi-episode clinical dataset

round_transcripts.critic:
Two critical issues undermine this design.

First, all preprocessing transformers — scaling, encoding, imputation, text vectorization, and image normalization — are fit on the entire dataset before splitting. The design explicitly states: 'All preprocessing transformers are fit using the entire dataset before splitting.' This is a systematic leakage of validation and test distribution information into all preprocessing steps applied during model training.

Second, the design uses a stratified random split for a 24-month clinical dataset that includes patients who may have multiple episodes. Without explicit patient-level grouping, encounters from the same patient will appear in both training and test sets, creating patient-level leakage that inflates test performance on a disease diagnosis task where patient-specific patterns (anatomy, chronic conditions, imaging style) can be highly predictive.
round_transcripts.defender:
On preprocessing leakage: fitting on the full dataset including image normalization using DICOM statistics ensures that all three data centers' imaging protocols are represented in the normalization parameters. Training-only normalization would underrepresent facilities that are overweighted in validation or test sets. However, the critic is correct that this practice causes leakage. The practical alternative is per-facility normalization statistics fit on training images from each facility, then applied to validation/test using the same facility's training statistics.

On patient-level leakage: the design does not explicitly address patient-level grouping. In a 24-month clinical dataset for multi-class disease diagnosis, repeat patient encounters are highly likely. Patient-level grouping by unique identifier must be enforced before splitting to prevent the model from recognizing patient-specific anatomical or clinical patterns. This is a genuine design gap.

round_transcripts.critic: Both issues are acknowledged by the defense as genuine flaws. No substantive resolution is offered for either concern.
round_transcripts.defender: Both issues are genuine. Preprocessing should use training-only or per-facility training statistics. Patient-level grouping must be enforced before splitting.

all_issues_adjudicated: All preprocessing transformers fit on full dataset — genuine leakage, acknowledged
Patient-level grouping not enforced — genuine leakage, acknowledged
